MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Stephen Tigerman, Esq., of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN, LLP (415-788-1588) for plaintiffs / opposing parties Beverlee Edwards, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Union Carbide Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. Stephen Hart's deposition testimony and declaration, Phillip Hart's deposition testimony and declaration, and Mr. Hamilton's 2008 trial testimony create a triable issue whether Mrs. Nine was exposed to Hamilton joint compound that contained asbestos attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Stephen Hart's declaration, Phillip Hart's declaration and Mr. Hamilton's trial testimony are overruled. Plaintiffs dismissed their enterprise liability and false representation causes of action, which were the only issues for which summary adjudication was sought. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR # 1480 = (220/HEK)
